title: Command Line Switches Reference
text: Command Line Switches Reference Command line switches can be used with Uniface executables to specify configuration files, run utilities, supply parameters, apply operating constraints, and otherwise qualify the session. They remain in effect for the duration of that application session. For more information, see Uniface Command Line Interface . The command line switches and sub-switches are described in the following topics. Related Links Uniface Command Line Interface Uniface provides a command line interface that enables you to access many functions without actually opening the user interface. By running a Uniface executable (such as ide.exe or uniface) with command line switches you can compile objects, debug applications, import and export, specify assignment files, and more.
